pattern: Two strings are interchangeable when the multiset of their even-indexed characters and the multiset of their odd-indexed characters both match; sorting each half produces a signature that identifies the equivalence class.
hints: You may freely rearrange characters at even positions among themselves, and characters at odd positions among themselves — this means only the multiset at each parity matters, not the order.
Build a signature for each word by sorting its even-indexed characters and sorting its odd-indexed characters separately, then combine them into a single comparable key.
Insert every signature into a set; the size of that set is the number of distinct equivalence classes.

edge_inputs.description: six words forming three groups via even/odd rearrangements
six permutations of three letters forming three groups
single word is always one group
all words identical — just one group
two-character words where even and odd each hold one letter
swapping even chars changes the group even when odd chars match
all words are in distinct groups
longer words where many even/odd combos coincide
repeated letters make even/odd multisets equal across different words

stress.description: random same-length words drawn from a small alphabet to create overlapping groups
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    length = rng.choice([2, 4, 6])
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        w = ''.join(rng.choice('abcd') for _ in range(length))
        words.append(w)
    return [words]
